title: Experience / Engineering Impact
introduction: This section highlights concrete engineering work, focusing on technical decisions and their impact rather than roles or titles.

experiences.problem: Needed a unified platform to manage personal projects, deployment pipelines, and documentation across multiple environments without relying on fragmented third-party tools that each required separate authentication and configuration.
decisions: Chose Next.js with App Router for server-first rendering and simplified API co-location
Implemented token-based authentication with JWT refresh rotation instead of session cookies for stateless horizontal scaling
Used PostgreSQL with row-level security policies to isolate multi-project data without separate databases
Containerized the entire stack with Docker Compose for reproducible local and production environments
Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD with staged deployment to preview and production targets
experiences.outcome: Consolidated five separate tools into one system. Deployment time dropped from manual 20-minute processes to automated 3-minute pipelines. All projects now share a single authentication layer.

experiences.problem: Built a real-time plant disease detection system for agricultural research. The constraint was running inference on low-bandwidth connections with images captured from mobile devices in field conditions with inconsistent lighting and resolution.
decisions: Selected a lightweight CNN architecture (MobileNetV2) over heavier models to maintain sub-second inference on constrained hardware
Implemented server-side image preprocessing pipeline to normalize lighting and resolution before classification
Designed a REST API with queued inference to handle burst uploads without blocking the main thread
Used SQLite for local caching of recent predictions to provide offline fallback functionality
experiences.outcome: Achieved 94% classification accuracy across 12 plant disease categories. The system handled 200+ daily field submissions with an average response time under 800ms.

experiences.problem: A small business needed an inventory and order management system to replace spreadsheet-based tracking that was causing duplicate orders and lost inventory records across three warehouse locations.
decisions: Designed a normalized relational schema with optimistic locking to prevent concurrent edit conflicts
Built role-based access control with three permission tiers: warehouse staff, managers, and administrators
Implemented real-time stock synchronization across locations using WebSocket connections
Chose server-side rendering for the dashboard to minimize client-side JavaScript and improve load times on older hardware used in warehouses
experiences.outcome: Eliminated duplicate order entries entirely. Inventory discrepancy rate dropped from approximately 8% to under 1%. Staff onboarding time reduced because the interface matched their existing mental model of the workflow.

experiences.problem: Designed a secure file-sharing system for a university network security course. The primary constraint was implementing end-to-end encryption without relying on any external encryption service or library beyond standard Web Crypto API.
decisions: Implemented AES-256-GCM encryption on the client side before upload to ensure zero-knowledge server architecture
Designed a key-exchange protocol using RSA-OAEP for secure sharing between authenticated users
Built chunked upload and download with integrity verification using SHA-256 checksums per chunk
Separated the authentication server from the file storage server to limit the blast radius of potential breaches
experiences.outcome: Passed all course security audit requirements. The system supported files up to 500MB with consistent encryption and decryption performance. Received highest grade in the cohort for architectural documentation.
